page: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 family, powered by an ARM-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. This server features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 236 GB of local storage. It is also capable of utilizing Premium Storage under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks position this instance primarily in the average tier for general computing tasks. The Neoverse-N2 architecture delivers average single-core and multi-core stress-ng performance, alongside average memory bandwidth speeds, reaching 454.75 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 90.31 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Notably, the processor demonstrates strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 33.1 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker results in specialized workloads, such as ray tracing, PassMark extended instructions, and cryptographic operations, where the encryption test scored in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a resource-dense profile that balances compute power and memory without the overhead of traditional x86 architectures. The inclusion of 236 GB of local storage adds value for workloads requiring fast temporary disk access. This server is well-suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database operations, though it is less optimal for intensive cryptographic tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, powered by an ARM-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores operating at 3.4 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 236 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers balanced performance, with benchmarks showing average results in general computing, database operations, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic and ray-tracing tasks. The ARM64 architecture offers a cost-efficient hardware profile for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources and local storage. This server is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database workloads.

tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory, and integrated local storage for standard workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 33.1 GB/sec
